title: Devices and methods for total internal reflection (tir) microscopy
abstract: According to a first aspect, the present description relates to a device for TIR microscopy of a sample (S) comprising a support (210) for receiving the sample; an illumination path comprising a spatially coherent light source (220), a microscope objective (230) and scanning means., at least a first detection path comprising said microscope objective and a first bidimensional optical acquisition device (240) and a computing device (250). The illumination path is configured to form a plurality of first illumination beam (I i ) for illuminating an interface (211) between said support and the sample, said first illumination beams having different azimuth angles (Ï i ) and a first polar angle (Î¸ 1 ) adapted for total internal reflection at said interface, each first illumination beam thus forming a first evanescent electromagnetic field (E Ei ) in the sample. The first detection path is configured for the acquisition of a plurality of interferograms (N i ), each interferogram resulting from the optical interference between at least a first reference electromagnetic field (E Ri ) and at least a first backscattered signal electromagnetic field (E Ei ), wherein said first reference electromagnetic field (E Ri ) results from the total internal reflection of one of said first illumination beams at said interface and said first backscattered signal electromagnetic field (E Si ) results from the backscattering by the sample of a first evanescent electromagnetic field (E Ei ) formed by said first illumination beam. The computing device (250) is configured to compute from said plurality of interferograms at least one a map of a physical parameter related to the sample.
claims:
Claims 1. A device for TIR microscopy of a sample (S) comprising: - a support (210) for receiving the sample; - an illumination path comprising a spatially coherent light source (220), a microscope objective (230) and scanning means, wherein said illumination path is configured to form a temporal sequence of first illumination beams (f) for illuminating an interface (211) between said support and the sample, wherein said first illumination beams ( I j) have different azimuth angles (f;) and at least a first polar angle (qi) adapted for total internal reflection at said interface, each first illumination beam thus forming a first evanescent electromagnetic field ( E E j) in the sample ; - at least a first detection path comprising said microscope objective and a first bidimensional optical acquisition device (240), wherein said first detection path is configured for the acquisition of a plurality of interferograms (Ni), each interferogram resulting from the optical interference between at least a first reference electromagnetic field (E R ;) and at least a first backscattered signal electromagnetic field (Esi), wherein said first reference electromagnetic field (E R ;) results from the total internal reflection of one of said first illumination beams at said interface and said first backscattered signal electromagnetic field (Esi) results from the backscattering by the sample of a first evanescent electromagnetic field (E E; ) formed by said first illumination beam; and - a computing device (250) configured to compute from said plurality of interferograms at least one map of a physical parameter related to the sample. 2. The device for TIR microscopy according to claim 1, wherein said at least one map of a physical parameter related to the sample comprises at least one of: a 2D map of the phase and/or modulus of a first backscattered signal electromagnetic field resulting from the backscattering by the sample of a first evanescent electromagnetic fields (E E; ) formed by one of said first illumination beams, a 2D map of an equivalent reflectivity r eq of the sample, a 3D map of a permittivity contrast De of the sample. 3. The device for TIR microscopy according to any of the preceding claims, further comprising a second detection path, wherein said second detection path comprises said microscope objective, a second bidimensional optical acquisition device (460) for acquisition of a fluorescence signal resulting from the fluorescence of said sample and a 

 beam splitter (461) configured to direct said fluorescence signal to said second bidimensional optical acquisition device. 4. The device for TIR microscopy according to any of the preceding claims, wherein: - said illumination path further comprises a beam shaper element (427) configured to form simultaneously for each of said first illumination beams (Iii) a second illumination beam (Ii 2 ), wherein said first and second illumination beams have the same polar angle and two azimuth angles spaced by 180Â°. 5. The device for TIR microscopy according to claim 4, wherein: - said first acquisition path further comprises a spatial filter (444) configured for blocking for each of said first illumination beams, one of first and second reflections beams (Rii, Ri 2 ), wherein said first and second reflections beams result respectively from the total internal reflection of said first and second illumination beams (Iii, Ii 2 ) at said interface (211) between the sample and the support. 6. A method for TIR microscopy of a sample (S) comprising the steps of: - illuminating with a temporal sequence of first illumination beams (T) an interface (211) between a support (210) and the sample using an illumination path comprising a spatially coherent light source (220), a microscope objective (230) and scanning means, wherein said first illumination beams (Â¾) have different azimuth angles and at least a first polar angle adapted for total internal reflection at said interface, each first illumination beam thus forming a first evanescent electromagnetic field (E E; ) in the sample ; - detecting, using a first detection path comprising said microscope objective and a first bidimensional optical acquisition device, a plurality of interferograms (Nj), each interferogram resulting from the optical interference between at least a first reference electromagnetic field (ER;) and at least a first backscattered signal electromagnetic field (Esi), wherein said first reference electromagnetic field (ER;) results from the total internal reflection of one of said first illumination beams at said interface and said first backscattered signal electromagnetic field (Esi) results from the backscattering by the sample of a first evanescent electromagnetic field formed by said first illumination beam; and - computing, from said plurality of interferograms, at least one map of a physical parameter related to the sample. 7. The method for TIR microscopy according to claim 6, wherein the computing step comprises: - performing a numerical 2D Fourier transform of at least one of said plurality of interferograms corresponding to a given illumination azimuth angle; - filtering said 2D Fourier transform to retrieve the 2D Fourier transform of said first backscattered signal electromagnetic field (Esi) corresponding to said illumination azimuth angle. 8. The method for TIR microscopy according to any of claims 6 to 7, wherein the computing step comprises performing a summation of said plurality of interferograms. 9. The method according to claim 8, wherein the computing step comprises: - performing a numerical 2D Fourier transform of each interferogram (Nj); - filtering said 2D Fourier transform to retrieve the 2D Fourier transform of the backscattered signal electromagnetic field (Esi) for each illumination azimuth angle; - summing said 2D Fourier transforms of the backscattered signal electromagnetic field (Esi) for all illumination azimuth angle; - performing an inverse 2D Fourier transform of said sum to obtain a 2D map of the phase and/or modulus of an equivalent reflectivity of the sample. 10. The method for TIR microscopy according to any of claims 6 to 9, further comprising acquiring using a second detection path, a fluorescence signal resulting from the fluorescence of the sample. 11. The method for TIR microscopy according to any of claims 6 to 10, wherein: - the illuminating step further comprises, for each first illumination beam, simultaneously illuminating said interface with said first illumination beam (In) and a second illumination beam ( ), wherein the first and second illumination beams have the same polar angle and two azimuth angles spaced by 180Â°. 12. The method for TIR microscopy according to claim 11 , wherein: - the detecting step further comprises blocking one of first and second reflections beams (Rii, Ri 2 ), resulting respectively from the total internal reflection of said first and 

 second illumination beams (Ii ls Ii 2 ) at said interface (211) between the sample and the support.
